name: Economy (Hard)
description: Aggressive fuel-saving mode. Target: -10%..-15% fuel consumption. Performance is intentionally reduced: peak torque ~370 Nm (-7%), peak power ~135 PS (-17%). The car feels deliberately sluggish - by design, for fuel. Suited for fleet vehicles, long-distance logistics, periods of high fuel prices. Feels too soft for urban use; most beneficial at highway cruise speed (90-110 km/h). EGR stays ON.

edits.comment: +5% lowest RPM band (1250-1500 rpm). Driver gets enough torque even at very low RPM, upshift reflex strengthens.
-7% peak torque band (1700-2500 rpm). Stock 400 Nm peak drops to ~370 Nm. Curve shape is preserved (whole plateau cut by same percentage), only the apex falls. Performance feel noticeably reduced.
-12% mid-upper RPM (2700-3300 rpm). If driver is on the pedal in this region, they're burning fuel; the torque cut forces them to drive more calmly.
-20% upper RPM (3500-4100 rpm). High-RPM power is severely capped. Driver gives up on revving for acceleration - the largest single contributor to the fuel-consumption curve.
+12% rail pressure across a wide region. Very fine fuel spray, more complete and efficient combustion. Cap 1400 bar (CP1 practical upper limit).
+5% boost low-mid RPM. Strong low-RPM torque support = early upshifts. Cap 2400 mbar (turbo-safe).
-5% boost upper RPM. Pedal-down feels turbo-starved - driver doesn't want to accelerate, calms down.

+4% main fuel in cruise band (1700-2300 rpm at moderate load). This is the highway cruise zone - small extra fuel combined with boost gives better cruise efficiency = less pumping loss from a more open throttle.
-10% main fuel at high RPM + high load. 'Acceleration' performance is markedly reduced - eco driving becomes effectively mandatory.
-15% transient fuel. When driver punches the pedal the engine feels almost 'unresponsive'. Classic eco-mode character.
-12% on the secondary transient map. Consistent eco character.

options.label: Keep EGR active (REQUIRED for Economy mode)
options.description: EGR is the heart of Economy mode. It recycles exhaust gas, lowers combustion temperature, reduces NOx production, and net-reduces fuel consumption. EGR-off made sense in Stage 1/Stage 2 (performance-focused, extra fuel needs to clear soot elsewhere); in Economy mode EGR-off zeroes the fuel saving. DO NOT enable.

options.label: Highway cruise optimisation
options.description: Optional: extra rail pressure and slight boost trim for the 80-110 km/h cruise band (~1900-2300 rpm at moderate load). Lowers consumption on the highway, no effect on urban driving. Enable for fleet / long-haul use.

edits.comment: Extra rail pressure in the highway cruise band.
